markets._comment: Ignore filler fights. Measured by traded volume, with SEPARATE floors for open and settled markets because volume accrues right up to the bell - one floor for both would filter by how soon a fight is, not how big.

boxing._why: Boxing looked worthless: 31 graded picks, +2.3% ROI vs the line, 77% of them favourites at an average of 73c. That is the exact signature of 'right a lot, earning nothing'. But it was composition, not talent. On a small card the matchmaking is protective, the favourite is a heavy favourite, everyone picks him, and there is nothing to disagree about. Filter to the fights that matter and ROI goes 2.3% -> ~19%. HONESTY: that is 12-14 picks. The direction is trustworthy (an absolute floor and a relative one both showed it, and it matches a real structural story). The magnitude is not. Also cuts La Velada, the Spanish influencer boxing event Kalshi files under KXBOXING, which no analyst on our roster covers.

mma._why: Deliberately unfiltered. Across thresholds MMA went 24.6% -> 10.0% -> 14.2%: non-monotonic, i.e. noise. There is no effect here to exploit, and picking the best-looking cut would be curve-fitting.
